question: Q.1 The killing of a king.
explanation: Regicide is the killing of a king. Homicide is killing any human; Patricide is killing one's father; Genocide is the mass killing of an ethnic group.

question: Q.2 A person who walks in their sleep.
explanation: Somnambulist (somnus=sleep + ambulare=walk) walks in sleep. Somniloquist talks in sleep; Truant skips school; Stoic is indifferent to pain.

question: Q.3 One who never drinks alcohol.
explanation: Teetotaller completely abstains from alcohol. Epicure/Gourmet enjoy fine food; Ascetic leads a strictly simple life avoiding all pleasures.

question: Q.4 The practice of having more than one husband at a time.
explanation: Polyandry (poly=many + andros=man) is having multiple husbands. Polygamy is multiple spouses; Bigamy is two marriages; Monogamy is one marriage.

question: Q.5 A person who collects coins.
explanation: Numismatist collects coins. Philatelist collects stamps; Connoisseur is an expert judge of art/food; Mercenary works only for money.

question: Q.6 The killing of one's own mother.
explanation: Matricide (matri=mother + cide=kill) is killing one's mother. Patricide is father; Fratricide is brother; Sororicide is sister.

question: Q.7 A person indifferent to pleasure or pain.
explanation: Stoic remains calm regardless of pain or pleasure. Epicure enjoys food; Ascetic avoids comfort for discipline; Egoist is self-centered.

question: Q.8 A student who stays away from school without permission.
explanation: Truant is a student who skips school. Somnambulist is a sleepwalker; Mercenary is money-driven; Ascetic is a hermit/monk-like person.

question: Q.9 The killing of an infant.
explanation: Infanticide is killing a baby. Homicide is any human; Uxoricide is killing one's wife; Regicide is killing a king.

question: Q.10 A critical judge of any art or food.
explanation: Connoisseur is an expert in arts/taste. Philatelist=stamps; Gourmet=food lover; Numismatist=coins.

question: Q.11 The practice of marrying only one person at a time.
explanation: Monogamy (mono=one) is one marriage. Bigamy=two; Polygamy=multiple wives/spouses; Polyandry=multiple husbands.

question: Q.12 Too powerful to be defeated.
explanation: Invincible cannot be defeated. Infallible never makes mistakes; Inevitable cannot be avoided; Incorrigible cannot be corrected.

question: Q.13 One who collects postage stamps.
explanation: Philatelist collects stamps. Numismatist collects coins; Epicure loves fine dining; Truant skips classes.

question: Q.14 The killing of one's own brother.
explanation: Fratricide (frater=brother) is killing a brother. Sororicide=sister; Matricide=mother; Patricide=father.

question: Q.15 A person who hates marriage.
explanation: Misogamist (miso=hate + gamos=marriage) hates marriage. Misanthrope hates mankind; Ascetic avoids pleasure; Stoic is indifferent to pain.

question: Q.16 Impossible to repair.
explanation: Irreparable cannot be fixed. Incorrigible cannot be reformed; Illegible cannot be read; Inaudible cannot be heard.

question: Q.17 The killing of one's own wife.
explanation: Uxoricide (uxor=wife) is killing one's wife. Regicide=king; Homicide=human; Infanticide=infant.

question: Q.18 One who talks in their sleep.
explanation: Somniloquist talks in sleep. Somnambulist walks in sleep; Teetotaller avoids alcohol; Truant avoids school.

question: Q.19 A person who leads a strictly simple life avoiding pleasures.
explanation: Ascetic lives without luxury for discipline. Epicure/Gourmet enjoy fine food; Stoic is calm during pain.

question: Q.20 The killing of one's own father.
explanation: Patricide (patri=father) is killing a father. Matricide=mother; Regicide=king; Genocide=ethnic group.

question: Q.21 The deliberate killing of a large ethnic group.
explanation: Genocide (genos=race) is mass race killing. Homicide=one person; Infanticide=baby; Uxoricide=wife.

question: Q.22 A connoisseur of good food.
explanation: Gourmet is an expert in high-quality food. Epicure also enjoys food; Stoic is emotionless; Teetotaller avoids alcohol.

question: Q.23 The offense of marrying someone while already married.
explanation: Bigamy is a second illegal marriage. Monogamy=one; Polygamy=multiple wives; Polyandry=multiple husbands.

question: Q.24 Someone who cannot be reformed.
explanation: Incorrigible (in=not + corrigere=correct) cannot be improved. Invincible=undefeatable; Infallible=error-free; Inevitable=unavoidable.

question: Q.25 The killing of one's own sister.
explanation: Sororicide (soror=sister) is killing a sister. Fratricide=brother; Uxoricide=wife; Matricide=mother.

question: Q.26 Abstinence from marriage for religious reasons.
explanation: Celibacy is remaining unmarried/chaste for religion. Monogamy/Bigamy/Polygamy are states of being married.

question: Q.27 The killing of a human by another.
explanation: Homicide is the general term for killing a human. Regicide=king; Genocide=race; Patricide=father.

question: Q.28 A person taking pleasure in fine food and drink.
explanation: Epicure is someone who loves luxury in food. Teetotaller avoids alcohol; Ascetic avoids pleasure; Stoic is indifferent to pain.

word: Q.29 Concerned with money over ethics.
explanation: Mercenary is driven purely by profit. Philanthropist loves humans/donates; Egoist is self-centered; Optimist is positive.

question: Q.30 Practice of having more than one wife at a time.
explanation: Polygamy is the general term for multiple spouses (usually wives). Monogamy=one; Polyandry=multiple husbands; Bigamy=two marriages.
